verses.text: Suddenly Elijah appears, declaring there will be no rain except by his word. Into the darkest reign God sends His boldest prophet. When evil seems triumphant, the LORD raises up one voice to confront a whole apostate kingdom — and shuts up heaven to prove who truly reigns.

God sends Elijah to hide by the brook Cherith, promising ravens will feed him there. The prophet is cared for in secret before his public work. God hides and provides for His servants in obscure places, preparing them in private for the trials ahead.

God sends Elijah to hide by the brook Cherith, promising ravens will feed him there. The prophet is cared for in secret before his public work. God hides and provides for His servants in obscure places, preparing them in private for the trials ahead.

God sends Elijah to hide by the brook Cherith, promising ravens will feed him there. The prophet is cared for in secret before his public work. God hides and provides for His servants in obscure places, preparing them in private for the trials ahead.

Elijah obeys, and ravens — unclean scavengers — bring him bread and meat morning and evening, until the brook dries up. God provides by the most unlikely means, then moves him on when the supply ends. He feeds His own creatively, never bound to one channel.

Elijah obeys, and ravens — unclean scavengers — bring him bread and meat morning and evening, until the brook dries up. God provides by the most unlikely means, then moves him on when the supply ends. He feeds His own creatively, never bound to one channel.

Elijah obeys, and ravens — unclean scavengers — bring him bread and meat morning and evening, until the brook dries up. God provides by the most unlikely means, then moves him on when the supply ends. He feeds His own creatively, never bound to one channel.

Sent to a widow in Zarephath, Elijah finds her gathering sticks for a last meal before she and her son die. He asks her, in her poverty, to feed him first. God leads His servant to the empty-handed, and tests a starving woman's faith with a startling request.

Sent to a widow in Zarephath, Elijah finds her gathering sticks for a last meal before she and her son die. He asks her, in her poverty, to feed him first. God leads His servant to the empty-handed, and tests a starving woman's faith with a startling request.

Elijah promises that if she gives first, her meal and oil will not run out till the rain returns — and so it proves, day after day. Faith that gives its last at God's word finds the supply renewed. The little surrendered to God outlasts the much we cling to.

Then the widow's son sickens and dies, and she cries that the prophet's coming has exposed her sin. Even sheltered by a miracle, grief breaks in. Faith's hardest trials sometimes follow its sweetest mercies, and the heart cries out in its confusion.

Then the widow's son sickens and dies, and she cries that the prophet's coming has exposed her sin. Even sheltered by a miracle, grief breaks in. Faith's hardest trials sometimes follow its sweetest mercies, and the heart cries out in its confusion.

Elijah carries the boy upstairs, pleads with God, and stretches himself on the child three times, and the LORD hears: the soul returns and the child lives. The prophet wrestles in prayer for the dead, and God answers. He is the God who gives life back when all hope is gone.

Elijah carries the boy upstairs, pleads with God, and stretches himself on the child three times, and the LORD hears: the soul returns and the child lives. The prophet wrestles in prayer for the dead, and God answers. He is the God who gives life back when all hope is gone.

Elijah carries the boy upstairs, pleads with God, and stretches himself on the child three times, and the LORD hears: the soul returns and the child lives. The prophet wrestles in prayer for the dead, and God answers. He is the God who gives life back when all hope is gone.

Elijah brings the living boy down, and the woman declares, 'now I know that thou art a man of God, and that the word of the LORD in thy mouth is truth.' The miracle seals her faith. God confirms His word by His works, that hearts may trust both His messenger and His message.
